components.role: Unity-gain buffer in Sallen-Key topology — provides the positive feedback path that creates resonance
First filter resistor — sets cutoff frequency with R2, C1, C2
Second filter resistor — equal to R1 for Butterworth response
First filter capacitor — shunt to ground before op-amp input
Second filter capacitor — feedback cap from output to R1/R2 junction; C2 = 2*C1 for Butterworth Q

dsp_model.description: Second-order IIR lowpass (biquad); -12dB/octave rolloff, Q controls resonance peak at cutoff

behavioral_description: A second-order active lowpass filter with a steeper rolloff than a simple RC filter (-12dB/octave vs -6dB/octave). The Sallen-Key topology uses an op-amp as a unity-gain buffer with a clever capacitor feedback path that creates a resonant second-order response. The Q parameter controls the shape at the cutoff frequency: at Q=0.707 (Butterworth), the response is maximally flat with no resonant peak. Higher Q values create a subtle boost at the cutoff before rolling off, adding presence and 'bite' just before the treble drops away. This is commonly used in pedals as a tone-shaping filter after distortion stages, or as the main filter in wah and envelope filter pedals. The steeper rolloff gives a more defined tonal character than a first-order filter.
